steps.title: Translate rates and times into distances
steps.content:
Distance = rate × time.
Evan drives at 45 mph for e hours $\Rightarrow$ distance = 45e miles.
Maya drives at 50 mph for m hours $\Rightarrow$ distance = 50m miles.
Total distance = 45e + 50m miles.

steps.title: Apply the total distance condition
steps.content:
The problem states the total distance was under 310 miles, so the total must be strictly less than 310:
$$45e + 50m \lt 310$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• "$45e+50m\gt 310$": Uses the correct coefficients but the wrong inequality direction — this would mean the total exceeds 310 miles. ✗
  • "$45e+50m\lt 310$": Evan’s distance (45e) plus Maya’s distance (50m) is less than 310 miles — exactly the given constraint. ✓
  • "$50e+45m\gt 310$": Swaps the speeds (assigns 50 to Evan and 45 to Maya) and uses the wrong inequality direction. ✗
  • "$50e+45m\lt 310$": Has the correct inequality direction but swaps Evan’s and Maya’s speeds. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
